text:
$IBKR – here’s how to get real-time market data for any international exchange on IBKR

After going deep on photonics bottleneck plays outside the U.S. (thanks Serenity), I kept running into the same problem: no real-time data. Japan, Taiwan, UK, France, Germany, Sweden ($SIVE right?) – all delayed or missing.

You have two options: buy at market price blind, or pay $1-2/month per exchange for live data.

I went with the latter. Here’s how to set it up.

Step-by-step guide:

1.Log into IBKR and go to Settings (top right, your profile icon)
https://t.co/wi6vysjrJb the Settings menu, tap Market Data Subscriptions
https://t.co/ieOXkA2beU’ll see your current subscriptions listed by region – North America, Europe, Asia Pacific
4.Tap Configure (the gear icon in the top right of the Subscriptions panel)
https://t.co/7lNGUKtQOx for the exchange you want – Taiwan Stock Exchange, Japan TSE, LSE, etc.
6.Add it. Most non-U.S. exchanges run $1-2 USD/month.
7.Confirm and you’ll get a “Thank You” screen once the subscription is active

Is it worth it? Depends on your position size.

If real-time data helps you save just 1% on entry, that’s $100-$1,000 back in your pocket depending on how much you’re deploying – which could be several years worth of the monthly fee in a single trade.

For me, it’s a no-brainer.

text:
RARE EARTH THEME: Setting up Huge 

Rare earth elements are the "vitamins" of the 2026 technology buildout. You only need a small amount, but without them, the high-performance hardware required for AI, photonics, and space infrastructure simply won't function.

They are the PHSYICAL BOTTLENECK for AI, As of 2026, China still refines over 90% of the world's supply. This has created a "stockpiling" environment as the U.S. and E.U. rush to bring non-Chinese refining facilities (like MP Materials in California) online before new defense bans take effect in 2027.

The Time Gap: While a data center can be built in months, opening a new mine and refinery often takes a decade. This mismatch between skyrocketing demand and slow supply growth is the primary reason for the "rerate" we're about to see. 

When a entire group is setting up, you pay attention.

LWLG — a real materials bet wrapped in a momentum stock NFA

Lightwave Logic makes Perkinamine electro-optic polymers that spin-coat onto silicon photonics wafers in a back-end-of-line step  (<100°C) to convert passive silicon waveguides into sub-volt, high-bandwidth Mach-Zehnder modulators. 

The technology’s figure of merit (Vπ·L ≈ 0.1 V·cm) is roughly an order of magnitude better than silicon plasma-dispersion modulators and competitive with thin-film lithium niobate. 

The relevance to AI is direct: 800G/1.6T/3.2T optical interconnects and co-packaged optics need exactly this — low-drive-voltage, 

BEOL-compatible, foundry-integrable modulators.
Commercial reality is far less mature. FY2025 revenue: $237 thousand   (yes, thousand) on a $2.45B market cap (P/S ~10,000x). Net loss $20.3M;  cash $69M  after  the December 2025 $35M offering at $3.10/share.  Management explicitly states no meaningful production revenue until 2027.   

The investor must accept this is a binary 2027 outcome. The bull case rests on GlobalFoundries PDK integration (March 2026), Tower Semiconductor tape-out, SilTerra/Luceda partnership, and four 

Fortune Global 500 customers in Stage 3 of the Design Win Cycle. Importantly, the Marvell-Polariton acquisition (closed 
February 2026,  ~$3.25B + $2.25B earnout)  creates a tantalizing but unconfirmed technical link — Polariton’s plasmonic-organic-hybrid demos historically used Perkinamine,  but Marvell has announced no LWLG license or supply agreement.  

Bulls are extrapolating; bears point out the company has been “near commercialization” since its PSI-TEC days in the 2000s.

text:
Biggest moves come from biggest stories.

- memory supercycle
- gov't stake in INTC
- photonics bottleneck
- war = big oil move
- software short (anthropic narrative)
+ plenty more

Some new(er) "stories" I'm watching:
- live commerce
- tokenization of assets
- new drone use-cases
- personalized health
- ai agents

Price *usually* shapes the narrative...but sometimes you can sniff out the future narratives before price even realizes what's happening.

I have followers across many industries, what are you guys sniffing out that nobody else realizes yet?

text:
$SITM my second blockbuster this season, after $STRL.
25% up on earnings!!!

I was a bit nervous because of the cost of Renesas acquisition in Feb. 

All the photonics chasers in X miss out this gem. I don't know how and why.

High precision clock is very important for synchronization in any digital communication -- between logic units (GPUs, CPUs, TPUs) for parallel computation and conversion of electrical signals to optical signals. That's right. There is a unit called DSP (digital signal processor) which takes binary electrical data from the logic units and transforms it into an electrical signal for light modulation unit which chops the CW light. For 800G/1.6T to work, it requires to handle sub-70 femtosecond jitter. Although $SWKS can achieve this, but in a convoluted way. $SITM's MEMS Resonators are tiny and work solid at 100C. Ideal for optical tranceivers and CPOs.

Another huge positive story is Renesas integration, where $SITM can vertically integrate this crucial part of the photonic fabrics. Instead of having a separate clock chip on a board, Renesas' microcontroller unit can have $SITM's clock integrated inside the package. With this, $SITM will be able to distribute the timing signal, clean jitters for 800G/1.6T networking, synchronize 5G/6G base stations. It will be unbeatable!
embedded_text:
$SITM is creating a very wide moat around high speed MEMS, I think.

EpiSeals, DualMEMS, plua 100+ patents owned by Luts
Now, adding all IPs from Renesas

Laser, Photonics, ... without ultrafast clocking doesn't make much sense for sure.

High precision clock is very important for synchronization in any digital communication -- between logic units (GPUs, CPUs, TPUs) for parallel computation and conversion of electrical signals to optical signals. That's right. There is a unit called DSP (digital signal processor) which takes binary electrical data from the logic units and transforms it into an electrical signal for light modulation unit which chops the CW light. For 800G/1.6T to work, it requires to handle sub-70 femtosecond jitter. Although $SWKS can achieve this, but in a convoluted way. $SITM's MEMS Resonators are tiny and work solid at 100C. Ideal for optical tranceivers and CPOs. Another huge positive story is Renesas integration, where $SITM can vertically integrate this crucial part of the photonic fabrics. Instead of having a separate clock chip on a board, Renesas' microcontroller unit can have $SITM's clock integrated inside the package. With this, $SITM will be able to distribute the timing signal, clean jitters for 800G/1.6T networking, synchronize 5G/6G base stations. Hard ≠ unique. If hyperscalers care, they won’t single-source a tiny cap such as $SIVE

That view misses what is already happening under the surface. This is a company that has shipped tens of millions of devices and is producing at scale today. This is not a lab concept or a maybe in five years technology, it is real manufacturing with real deployment and real customers. What becomes even more interesting is where they sit in the stack. As AI infrastructure scales, the industry is running into hard physical limits, power, heat, bandwidth. One of the key architectural shifts being explored is the move toward external laser sources for optical interconnects. That is exactly where Sivers is positioned. If that approach becomes standard, they are not just another supplier, they become part of a critical bottleneck layer in next generation systems. And this is not easy technology to replicate. We are talking about sub micron manufacturing precision and extremely tight optical performance tolerances. That kind of capability is built over many years, and only a small number of companies globally can actually execute at that level. On top of that, one of the biggest risks in photonics, scaling production, has already been addressed. Through partnerships with foundries like WIN Semiconductor, there is already a path toward high volume manufacturing. That removes a major uncertainty that holds many companies in this space back. At the same time, they are quietly embedded in the global photonics ecosystem. Collaborations with research leaders like IMEC place them inside the same innovation network that feeds the broader semiconductor industry. That kind of positioning often means early access to what comes next and a role in shaping it. Put all of this together and the picture looks very different from the usual speculative small cap narrative. It starts to look more like a scaled and technically entrenched player sitting right at the intersection of where AI infrastructure is heading next."}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2050272539851821157"}}, "rest_id": "2050305940516204613", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "20516", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2050316023203590144", "source": "Twitter for Android", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "506", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
🧠 Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ | Forensic X-Post
Canonical Ledger Entry

Title: Utility Truck with Roof-Mounted Light Array — Photonics / LiDAR Plausibility Assessment
Timestamp: March 23, 2021 | Santa Barbara, CA (702)
Tags: #Photonics #LiDAR #UtilityVehicles #Optics #PerceptionVsReality #ForensicAnalysis
Forensic Category: Visual Assessment / Technology Plausibility

⸻

⚖️ Core Proposition

Not all high-intensity light arrays or mounted equipment indicate advanced sensing systems. However, certain configurations can overlap with technologies used in mapping, scanning, and illumination.

The task is to separate:
👉 What is commonly deployed
from
👉 What is theoretically adaptable

⸻

🔍 Observed in Image

Pickup truck with roof-mounted light bar + auxiliary lighting cluster
Trailer-mounted equipment consistent with a wood chipper / landscaping machinery
Residential driveway setting
No visibly distinct sensor housings (e.g., spinning LiDAR dome, multi-lens rig, stabilized gimbal)

⸻

🔁 Effect → Cause Analysis

Observed Effect:
Large, multi-module light array mounted above cab

Most Likely Cause (Common Use):

High-lumen LED light bars used for:
Night work visibility
Landscaping / construction operations
Off-road or utility illumination

👉 These systems can appear intense on camera due to:

Lens compression
Reflective surfaces
Sensor bloom

⸻

Secondary Consideration (Technically Plausible, but Not Evident Here):

LiDAR / photonic systems typically require:

Dedicated sensor units (often cylindrical or box modules)
Clear line-of-sight mounting
Stabilization or calibration hardware
Power + data integration (visible cabling or mounts)

👉 None of those are clearly visible in this image.

⸻

⚠️ Important Distinction

A light bar ≠ LiDAR system

However:

Some industrial vehicles can carry both lighting + sensing equipment
Advanced mapping rigs (surveying, autonomous testing) use:
LiDAR
RGB cameras
IR sensors
        —but these are visually distinct when present

⸻

🧠 Conclusion

👉 Based on visible evidence, this appears to be a standard utility/work truck with high-output lighting, not a dedicated photonic or LiDAR deployment system.

👉 While dual-use adaptation is theoretically possible in broader contexts, there is no clear visual confirmation of LiDAR or advanced sensing hardware in this instance.

⸻

📌 Provenance Note

Visual interpretation grounded in:

Known vehicle equipment configurations
Optical behavior of high-intensity LEDs
Standard LiDAR hardware characteristics

⸻

text:
Massive YTD so far for PHOTONICS: The next layer of the AI buildout.
AI isn't just a compute story anymore.
It's becoming a data movement problem.
And the market is starting to price that in.
Why photonics is moving
More GPUs → more nodes → more data moving between them.
Copper can't keep up. Too much heat, too much power, wrong distances.
Photonics is the fix. And the buildout is already underway.
What the data is showing
$AAOI revenue grew 34% YoY last quarter — not speculation, actual orders.
800G and 1.6T optical capacity is being expanded right now to meet hyperscaler demand.
Earnings this week are the tell
$LITE — May 5 (after close)
$COHR — May 6 (after close)
$AAOI — May 7
If guidance on 800G/1.6T demand holds or accelerates, this thesis gets legs into 2027.
If hyperscaler commentary disappoints — the chart above gets a reality check.
Watch the guidance, that will be the clue. 
#photonics

Earnings this week are the tell $LITE — May 5 (after close) $COHR — May 6 (after close) $AAOI — May 7 If guidance on 800G/1.6T demand holds or accelerates, this thesis gets legs into 2027. text:
Sivers Semiconductors ($SIVE) is a small-cap player with real momentum: FY2025 revenue SEK 304M (+25% YoY), $453M pipeline, and production ramps in AI photonics, LiDAR (potential $28-53M cumulative), and SATCOM/defense deals. It's 50x smaller than $LITE (~$68B mcap, $2.1B TTM rev) or $COHR (~$62B mcap, multi-billion scale).

That size difference creates higher torque if execution hits—but also higher risk: ongoing losses, execution hurdles, competition from scaled peers, and valuation sensitivity. The big caps are more mature with broader customer bases.

Data backs the growth case for SIVE, yet skeptics rightly note small-caps often fail to deliver. DYOR; this isn't advice.

My core holdings (75% of portfolio):

Photonics — $MRVL, SIVE, AAOI, TSM, AEHR, IQE
Memory — MU, SNDK, SK Hynix
CPUs — ARM, INTC, AMD
Foundry — Win Semi

New longs I'm watching: POET, LPKF, M7U, NVTS, PDFS

Most of my research here is around photonics, memory, and AI infra bottlenecks. But my actual portfolio is broader than what you see on this account.

text:
🚀 $SIVE Price Targets: Week / Month / Year (Top 3 Catalysts Driving It)

$SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors) has absolutely exploded — up ~815% YTD and trading at ~38 SEK after hitting a 52-week high of 44.70 SEK. This photonics/laser play (wireless, optical comms, LiDAR, CPO for AI data centers) is riding serious momentum. Here’s my take on realistic price targets based on the top 3 near-term catalysts:

1. Nasdaq New York Dual Listing (Biggest Near-Term Catalyst)
•  Announced April 16, 2026 — they’re doing the PCAOB audit uplift and delayed the 2025 annual report to May 15 to prep for it (while staying domiciled in Sweden).
•  This opens the floodgates to US tech capital, higher liquidity, and a potential valuation rerating (US multiples love photonics/AI plays).
•  Impact: Listing progress/news could be the spark.

2. Major LiDAR Ramp (Q4 2026 Production Start)
•  Strategic customer integrating Sivers’ lasers + optical amplifiers across multiple platforms.
•  Revenue potential: $53M–$138M cumulative over the product lifecycle (auto + industrial). Ramp begins Q4 2026.
•  This is real, high-volume revenue coming online.

3. CPO / AI Data Center Supercycle (Longer-Term Rocket Fuel)
•  Sivers is a leader in external laser sources for Co-Packaged Optics (CPO).
•  Recent Jabil collab on 1.6T LRO transceivers for AI data centers + 64% pipeline growth to $453M. Tied directly into the NVDA/AVGO optical boom.
My speculative price targets (NFA — this thing is volatile as hell, high P/S, execution risk):

•  Next Week: 40–45 SEK (Momentum carry + any listing/earnings buzz. Watch for pullbacks after the recent spike.)

•  Next Month: 50–65 SEK (Dual-listing updates + continued AI/photonics hype. Could test new highs if volume stays strong.)

•  Next Year (End 2026 / into 2027): 90–130+ SEK (Dual listing complete + LiDAR ramp kicking off + CPO orders scaling. Market cap could easily 2–3x from here on execution.)

This is a high-conviction, high-risk name — asymmetric upside if the catalysts hit, but it’s already priced in a ton of optimism. Fundamentals are still catching up (still loss-making), but the narrative is strong in the AI/optics supercycle.

Position size accordingly and DYOR. What’s your $SIVE target? 

Holding long or swinging the volatility? 🔥

(Prices in SEK. Current ~38 as of late April close. Not financial advice.)

Everything looks to be going to plan. Another situation with these photonics stocks where listening to management talk is way more important than the numbers. This is a forward growth story about becoming "a top 3 global laser supplier" with the ramp not starting until H2. The company raised full year guidance to $1.1B from $1B. Talked about their 900,00 sq. ft in U.S. factory space doing up to 30% production in USA on top of their 700K+ Taiwanese space Discussed their CPO laser which they plan to 80x production of (said they're in talks with several customers on this) Said margins could get to 40% towards end of year up from ~30% Working with 3 hyperscalers, one qualified, another almost qualified. Lin said real demand is $1.4-$1.5B even though they can only fulfill $1.1B. The company re-affirmed their target of hitting $450 monthly revenue in June/July 2027 which would imply an annual revenue run rate of $5B+ at a current market cap of ~$12B. This is what has triggered the massive re-rate. Execution risk remains crazy high but management just doubled down on what they previously said which is why the stock trade as high as $190 recently. I just don't think the strategically important infrastructure of AAOI (domestic laser fabs, vertical integration, CPO laser) is being given enough credit here. This could easily still be a multi-bagger if they execute, with the possibility of being worth several tens of billions in the next few years hence why it's the largest position in my photonics fund."}}}, "rest_id": "2052509803302555865", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "36635", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2052528433226526947", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "53", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

It is rewarding the light layer too. Photonics is moving closer to the center of the machine.

#ArtificialIntelligence #DataCenters #Photonics #Semiconductors #Technology

Storage + interconnect - The plumbing hyperscalers can't build fast enough:

LITE → https://t.co/eJuFojKDem — photonics for inter-rack fabric
SNDK → https://t.co/MB8jqMwYD1 — NAND for inference checkpoints

COHR may be one of the most important AI infrastructure companies most investors still don’t fully understand.

Not GPUs. Not ASICs. Not memory.

Photonics.

Coherent sits at the physical layer of the AI buildout — the optical spine connecting GPUs, switches, and data centers.

Q3 FY26 revenue hit a record $1.81B, datacenter revenue grew 37% YoY, and NVIDIA just wrote a $2B check to secure the roadmap.

The bull case: 6-inch InP fabs, 800G → 1.6T → CPO, and a $23B growth SAM.

The bear case: debt, valuation, and execution risk.

Full deep dive below.

"to_index": 3582}, {"from_index": 3651, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 3685}]}, "text": "When you follow $SIVE closely over time, one thing that stands out is how many parallel tracks the company is advancing simultaneously. It rarely feels like a story built around a single product or market. Instead, it is a platform company where the same core photonics technology is applied across large and growing domains. One update that highlighted this breadth came on March 11, 2026, when $SIVE announced that a strategic LiDAR customer had chosen to integrate its photonics technology across all platforms. Production ramp is scheduled to begin in Q4 2026, targeting automotive and industrial applications. This is not a limited component agreement. $SIVE is supplying high-power continuous wave DFB laser diode arrays together with optical amplifiers integrated throughout the customer’s portfolio. Estimated cumulative lifetime revenue potential is $53–138 million, based on known volumes today, with clear upside as the customer scales. All indications point to this customer being $AEVA. Their 4D FMCW LiDAR technology, used in Atlas and X1, is engineered for long range, precise velocity measurement, and high resolution — capabilities required for real-world autonomous systems where safety and reliability are critical. $SIVE’s lasers help enable that performance, and their implementation across all platforms demonstrates the strength of $SIVE’s III-V photonics platform. It is worth stepping back and reflecting on what this means. The same technology stack powering highly efficient 1.6T transceivers with Jabil for AI data center infrastructure is now also finding its way into LiDAR sensors for trucks, passenger vehicles, robotics, and industrial automation. This ability to leverage one core competence across end-markets is one of the most compelling aspects of the $SIVE investment case. It lowers development risk, creates multiple growth engines, and allows ramps to mature at their own pace. $AEVA already has notable commercial momentum. They are integrated into $NVDA DRIVE Hyperion with volume production targeted for 2028 and have a partnership with Daimler Truck. As more OEMs, fleet operators, and robotics companies adopt similar LiDAR solutions, each new design win or deployment can drive higher shipment volumes. Automotive platforms often run for 8–10 years or longer, while industrial and robotics applications can create more stable long-term revenue streams. That gives $SIVE substantial room to grow beyond today’s base-case estimate. In the current market discussion, much focus is understandably on nearer-term opportunities — FWA and mmWave with Tachyon, or SATCOM progress through https://t.co/A7mlfHMHLp and $YSS. Those areas are important and may generate revenue momentum earlier. But the LiDAR ramp is a meaningful and underappreciated building block for 2027 and beyond. It does not begin as a small proof-of-concept. It starts with a strategic customer embedding $SIVE technology deeply from the beginning. This kind of multi-domain execution is what gives $SIVE its resilience and long-term potential. Photonics is the common thread connecting AI infrastructure (CPO), wireless connectivity (mmWave/FWA), satellite communications, and autonomous perception systems (LiDAR). With a reported pipeline exceeding $453 million and several parallel ramps in different stages of maturity, the company is not overly dependent on any single market or customer. The LiDAR opportunity still feels underappreciated. It may not dominate the daily discussion, but it starts contributing real production volumes already in Q4 this year. And as $AEVA expands in automotive and industrial markets, $SIVE’s participation across all platforms positions the company to capture a growing share of that opportunity. It is a reminder of why the longer-term thesis around $SIVE remains compelling: a specialized photonics capability that can scale across multiple high-growth verticals, creating revenue streams with significant long-term compounding potential."}}}, "rest_id": "2052353159398392187", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "515272", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2052515063945199683", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2320", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

PC에서 파워가 전력을 만들고, 메인보드·그래픽카드·RAM이 그 전력을 나눠 쓰듯이, 포토닉스 링크에서는 Laser가 만든 Optical Power가 Coupler → Waveguide → Modulator → Waveguide → PD로 이동한다. 문제는 이 과정에서 계속 Loss가 발생한다는 것이다. 보통 Laser Power는 mW가 아니라 dBm으로 본다. 쉽게 말하면, 100mW = 20 dBm 400mW = 26 dBm 이다. 400mW Laser는 100mW보다 출력은 4배 강하지만, dBm 기준으로는 +6 dB 높은 Laser다. 예를 들어 Laser 출력이 10 dBm이라고 해보자. Coupler에서 2 dB, Waveguide에서 2 dB, Modulator에서 3 dB 손실이 나면, PD에 도착하는 Optical Power는 결국 3 dBm 수준이다. 즉, 앞에서 아무리 강한 Laser를 넣어도 중간 Loss가 크면 PD가 받아들이는 Power는 크게 줄어든다. 반대로 말하면, Laser 자체가 무조건 강해야 좋은 시스템이 되는 것도 아니다. Laser에서 PD까지 가는 경로의 Loss가 낮으면, 더 낮은 Laser Power로도 충분히 쓸 수 있다. 그래서 포토닉스에서 중요한 질문은 단순히 “Laser가 얼마나 세냐?”가 아니다. 진짜 핵심은 이거다. 그 Laser Power를 PD까지 얼마나 덜 잃고 전달하느냐. 결국 Photonics는 Laser 싸움이면서 동시에 dB Loss 싸움이다."}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2051926679506088437"}}, "rest_id": "2052017338346885248", "source": "Twitter for Android", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "45805", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2052513391898739130", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "617", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Anyone else grab $AAOI in the 130's?

Everything looks to be going to plan.

Another situation with these photonics stocks where listening to management talk is way more important than the numbers.

This is a forward growth story about becoming "a top 3 global laser supplier" with the ramp not starting until H2.

The company raised full year guidance to $1.1B from $1B.
Talked about their 900,00 sq. ft in U.S. factory space doing up to 30% production in USA on top of their 700K+ Taiwanese space
Discussed their CPO laser which they plan to 80x production of (said they're in talks with several customers on this)
Said margins could get to 40% towards end of year up from ~30%
Working with 3 hyperscalers, one qualified, another almost qualified.
Lin said real demand is $1.4-$1.5B even though they can only fulfill $1.1B.

The company re-affirmed their target of hitting $450 monthly revenue in June/July 2027 which would imply an annual revenue run rate of $5B+ at a current market cap of ~$12B. This is what has triggered the massive re-rate.

Execution risk remains crazy high but management just doubled down on what they previously said which is why the stock trade as high as $190 recently. 

I just don't think the strategically important infrastructure of AAOI (domestic laser fabs, vertical integration, CPO laser) is being given enough credit here. 

This could easily still be a multi-bagger if they execute, with the possibility of being worth several tens of billions in the next few years hence why it's the largest position in my photonics fund.

text: StepStone platform manager @MayfieldFund participated in a $50M Series A-1 funding round for #OpenLight, a photonics company developing custom photonic application-specific integrated circuits for AI data centers and high-performance computing. This funding enables expansion of its photonic design kit, advances development of next-generation components including modulators and on-chip lasers and scales global sales and technical support infrastructure to accelerate deployment of its silicon photonics platform. text:
Cross-entropy at light speed? Nonlinear log is the killer—pure photonics struggles there without detection. 

Idea: Capture softmax probs as intensities on a detector array, then pipe into nonlinear optical elements (saturable absorbers or SOA chips) to approximate the log via intensity-dependent transmission. Modulate one-hot labels onto a parallel laser beam, interfere/combine for the -y log(p) products, and sum optically via passive couplers. 

Feasible in theory with wave-mixing crystals, but noisy and low-precision vs hybrid (optics forward, electronics loss). Early optical NN papers hint at approximations like this. What part are you optimizing for?

text:
The next $SIVE is probably in South Korea

I missed the $SIVE and the broader photonics run. By the time I finished my DD after seeing @aleabitoreddit's recommendation, it was already too late as it ran too high too fast.

Since then I have been scouring the market for similar opportunities that hasn't run as much as it did.

Specifically, I went hunting in Korea. Cheaper market, less analyst noise, and small-caps that the West hasn't gotten around to mispricing yet.

That's where I found OE Solutions. KOSDAQ ticker $138080.KQ. A Korean optical transceiver company that, the more I dug, kept giving me the strange feeling that I was reading an earlier version of the Sivers thesis--except this one already runs a fab at volume, ships product to named telecom customers, and trades at a fraction of the multiple.

A few things I now believe to be true after re-checking each line against primary sources where I could.

OE Solutions did about KRW 57.4bn in 2025 revenue (~$40m). Sivers reported SEK 304.1m in full-year 2025 revenue (~$30m). So the smaller-cap company does more revenue.

Market caps. As of early May 2026, Sivers sits at roughly SEK 15-16.5bn (~$1.5-1.7bn). OE Solutions sits at roughly KRW 458bn (~$340m at current FX). So OE has higher revenue at roughly one-fifth of Sivers' market cap. The price-to-sales gap is the headline.

Sell-side expects OE to swing to operating profit in 2026. Hana Securities is the most explicit on this--target raised from KRW 30,000 to KRW 50,000, "buy" rating maintained. Sivers, on consensus, is still loss-making in 2026. Treat OE's profit turn as sell-side opinion, not company guidance, but the directional gap is real.

Both companies make Indium Phosphide lasers, the enabling component for high-speed optical interconnects in AI data centers. The right framing isn't "chokepoint material"--it's that scaled InP suppliers are few, wafer yields are constrained, and demand is running ahead of capacity.

Where the two companies sit in the stack is different.

Sivers operates a 100mm InP wafer fab in Glasgow (acquired via CST Global in 2017). According to Sivers' own materials, the facility ships around 1 million lasers per month, with over 45 million lasers cumulatively in the field. They are not a small-scale supplier. To scale further for AI data center volumes, Sivers announced an OEM partnership with WIN Semiconductors in Taoyuan in March 2025--an outsourced foundry arrangement to expand DFB laser output. So the capacity story is "established volume supplier scaling further."

OE Solutions, per its own 2024 IR deck, runs vertical integration from epitaxy through chip fabrication to transceiver assembly out of its Gwangju facility. Korean industry reporting describes OE as mass-producing chips today and using its own laser diodes inside its own transceivers--with secondary reporting suggesting external chip sales to Chinese customers since 2024. I could not find a primary disclosure that puts an exact wafer-per-month number on the OE fab. So treat the comparison as: Sivers ships components into other people's modules, OE makes the modules itself. Different position in the stack, both with their own fabs.

Customers. Sivers has named, primary-source partnerships: Jabil for a 1.6T LRO transceiver collaboration announced April 15, 2026; POET Technologies for external light source modules, with prototypes targeted for H1 2026 and production-readiness for end-2026 (per Sivers' own Q4 2025 report, qualification with POET is ongoing); Ayar Labs going back to ECOC 2022; https://t.co/VKc0iGZ1CB in SATCOM. Most are real but development-stage.

OE Solutions is named in Korean industry reporting as supplying Samsung, Nokia, Ericsson, Cisco and Ciena, with NTT-bound FTTH volume coming through a Sumitomo handover. There are reports from Korea saying OE began mass-supplying 10G EPON OLT transceivers for NTT. I could not find a clean OE press release proving every one of those names is currently shipping, so the right characterization is "named in industry reporting as a customer of," not "verified invoice-paying." That said, the 2025 revenue jump of nearly 80% YoY needs to come from somewhere--it's not coming from thin air.

Both companies are positioned for the 1.6T transition. Sivers via the Jabil collaboration. OE via a 23 dBm cooled ELSFP module for CPO external light sources, with sampling targeted for Q3 2026 (this one is on OE's own blog as a primary source). Korean reporting also mentions a 1.6T OSFP unveiled at OFC 2026, but I couldn't surface OE's own press release for that exact product, so I'd hedge that line.

Sivers' stock is up roughly 1200%+ over the past twelve months. The thesis the market paid for is "InP laser scarcity, scaling capacity, AI data center exposure." That thesis worked.

The OE thesis rhymes. Same InP laser scarcity. Vertical integration that Sivers is now pursuing through outsourced partners. Earnings inflection driven by something more concrete than a hyperscaler win--the Sumitomo to NTT FTTH volume handover, which Korean industry reporting describes as roughly one-third reflected in current numbers, with the rest expected to flow through over the next several quarters. Treat that one-third figure as secondary reporting, not a number from a filing.

So why does the gap exist?

Sivers is European-listed and pursuing a Nasdaq dual listing--Western analyst coverage, English-language Substack writeups, the whole machinery. OE Solutions trades on KOSDAQ. The Korean small-cap discount is real and persistent. OE's investor disclosures page also shows repeated CB conversion entries through March-April 2026, which is exactly the dilution overhang you'd expect to weigh on the multiple.

These are real risks. I'm not waving them away.

But the valuation gap is too wide for the operational gap.

If OE Solutions gets even half of Sivers' price-to-sales multiple, applied to a revenue base that grows the way sell-side expects, you're looking at multiples of the current market cap. Pure rerating math. No new news required. And if the AI data center qualifications already in flight start landing--ITLA modules with Photon Bridge, the ELSFP sample shipments, and whatever materializes at the higher data rates--the rerating compounds.

The asymmetry is what matters. The Sumitomo handover and the laser diode internalization carry the stock to a meaningful re-rate without anything heroic happening. The data center catalyst is a free option on top of that. If it hits, it hits the way Sivers did. If it doesn't, the earnings inflection still pays you.

I missed Sivers. At Korean market open, I opened a position in OE Solutions.

Not financial advice. Do your own due diligence.

I missed the $SIVE and the broader photonics run. By the time I finished my DD after seeing @aleabitoreddit's recommendation, it was already too late as it ran too high too fast.

Since then I have been scouring the market for similar opportunities that hasn't run as much as it did.

Specifically, I went hunting in Korea. Cheaper market, less analyst noise, and small-caps that the West hasn't gotten around to mispricing yet.

That's where I found OE Solutions. KOSDAQ ticker $138080.KQ. A Korean optical transceiver company that, the more I dug, kept giving me the strange feeling that I was reading an earlier version of the Sivers thesis--except this one already runs a fab at volume, ships product to named telecom customers, and trades at a fraction of the multiple.

A few things I now believe to be true after re-checking each line against primary sources where I could.

OE Solutions did about KRW 57.4bn in 2025 revenue (~$40m). Sivers reported SEK 304.1m in full-year 2025 revenue (~$30m). So the smaller-cap company does more revenue.

Market caps. As of early May 2026, Sivers sits at roughly SEK 15-16.5bn (~$1.5-1.7bn). OE Solutions sits at roughly KRW 458bn (~$340m at current FX). So OE has higher revenue at roughly one-fifth of Sivers' market cap. The price-to-sales gap is the headline.

Sell-side expects OE to swing to operating profit in 2026. Hana Securities is the most explicit on this--target raised from KRW 30,000 to KRW 50,000, "buy" rating maintained. Sivers, on consensus, is still loss-making in 2026. Treat OE's profit turn as sell-side opinion, not company guidance, but the directional gap is real.

Both companies make Indium Phosphide lasers, the enabling component for high-speed optical interconnects in AI data centers. The right framing isn't "chokepoint material"--it's that scaled InP suppliers are few, wafer yields are constrained, and demand is running ahead of capacity.

Where the two companies sit in the stack is different.

Sivers operates a 100mm InP wafer fab in Glasgow (acquired via CST Global in 2017). According to Sivers' own materials, the facility ships around 1 million lasers per month, with over 45 million lasers cumulatively in the field. They are not a small-scale supplier. To scale further for AI data center volumes, Sivers announced an OEM partnership with WIN Semiconductors in Taoyuan in March 2025--an outsourced foundry arrangement to expand DFB laser output. So the capacity story is "established volume supplier scaling further."

OE Solutions, per its own 2024 IR deck, runs vertical integration from epitaxy through chip fabrication to transceiver assembly out of its Gwangju facility. Korean industry reporting describes OE as mass-producing chips today and using its own laser diodes inside its own transceivers--with secondary reporting suggesting external chip sales to Chinese customers since 2024. I could not find a primary disclosure that puts an exact wafer-per-month number on the OE fab. So treat the comparison as: Sivers ships components into other people's modules, OE makes the modules itself. Different position in the stack, both with their own fabs.

Customers. Sivers has named, primary-source partnerships: Jabil for a 1.6T LRO transceiver collaboration announced April 15, 2026; POET Technologies for external light source modules, with prototypes targeted for H1 2026 and production-readiness for end-2026 (per Sivers' own Q4 2025 report, qualification with POET is ongoing); Ayar Labs going back to ECOC 2022; https://t.co/VKc0iGZ1CB in SATCOM. Most are real but development-stage.

OE Solutions is named in Korean industry reporting as supplying Samsung, Nokia, Ericsson, Cisco and Ciena, with NTT-bound FTTH volume coming through a Sumitomo handover. There are reports from Korea saying OE began mass-supplying 10G EPON OLT transceivers for NTT. I could not find a clean OE press release proving every one of those names is currently shipping, so the right characterization is "named in industry reporting as a customer of," not "verified invoice-paying." That said, the 2025 revenue jump of nearly 80% YoY needs to come from somewhere--it's not coming from thin air.

Both companies are positioned for the 1.6T transition. Sivers via the Jabil collaboration. OE via a 23 dBm cooled ELSFP module for CPO external light sources, with sampling targeted for Q3 2026 (this one is on OE's own blog as a primary source). Korean reporting also mentions a 1.6T OSFP unveiled at OFC 2026, but I couldn't surface OE's own press release for that exact product, so I'd hedge that line.

Sivers' stock is up roughly 1200%+ over the past twelve months. The thesis the market paid for is "InP laser scarcity, scaling capacity, AI data center exposure." That thesis worked.

The OE thesis rhymes. Same InP laser scarcity. Vertical integration that Sivers is now pursuing through outsourced partners. Earnings inflection driven by something more concrete than a hyperscaler win--the Sumitomo to NTT FTTH volume handover, which Korean industry reporting describes as roughly one-third reflected in current numbers, with the rest expected to flow through over the next several quarters. Treat that one-third figure as secondary reporting, not a number from a filing.

So why does the gap exist?

Sivers is European-listed and pursuing a Nasdaq dual listing--Western analyst coverage, English-language Substack writeups, the whole machinery. OE Solutions trades on KOSDAQ. The Korean small-cap discount is real and persistent. OE's investor disclosures page also shows repeated CB conversion entries through March-April 2026, which is exactly the dilution overhang you'd expect to weigh on the multiple.

These are real risks. I'm not waving them away.

But the valuation gap is too wide for the operational gap.

If OE Solutions gets even half of Sivers' price-to-sales multiple, applied to a revenue base that grows the way sell-side expects, you're looking at multiples of the current market cap. Pure rerating math. No new news required. And if the AI data center qualifications already in flight start landing--ITLA modules with Photon Bridge, the ELSFP sample shipments, and whatever materializes at the higher data rates--the rerating compounds.

The asymmetry is what matters. The Sumitomo handover and the laser diode internalization carry the stock to a meaningful re-rate without anything heroic happening. The data center catalyst is a free option on top of that. If it hits, it hits the way Sivers did. If it doesn't, the earnings inflection still pays you.

I missed Sivers. At Korean market open, I opened a position in OE Solutions.

Not financial advice. Do your own due diligence."}}}, "rest_id": "2051422076989505927", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "10885", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
deep dive:

The tech is real. The management is radioactive. Here's why this is the most important bear case in photonics right now.

POET Technologies makes optical interposers — silicon wafers that let you snap photonic components into place without manual alignment. That's a 75% cost reduction on the most expensive part of building optical transceivers. If it works at scale, it's a real bottleneck-solver for AI data centers.

The problem isn't the engineering. It's everything else.

Revenue: $1.07 million trailing twelve months. That's not a typo. Q4 revenue was $341,202 — all prototype fees, zero product revenue. The company lost $42.7 million that same quarter. EBITDA is negative $39 million.

Yet the market cap is over $1 billion. That's 700x+ EV/revenue. For context, Marvell trades at 15-20x, Lumentum at 9-15x, Coherent at 9x. POET is priced like a scaled platform company that doesn't exist yet.

The cash position is real — $430 million after massive dilution (they raised $525 million in the last two quarters). So no bankruptcy risk. But the dilution story is brutal: they funded a pre-revenue R&D lab by printing shares, which created the PFIC tax problem.

Here's where it gets ugly. On April 14, Wolfpack Research published a short report flagging POET as a likely Passive Foreign Investment Company under US tax law — punitive tax treatment for American shareholders. The CFO called it a "nothing burger" on a YouTube livestream.

Two days later, he publicly disclosed Marvell's confidential purchase orders on that same YouTube channel. Marvell formally canceled all contracts on April 23, citing breach of NDA. That was POET's most validated commercial pipeline — gone in 48 hours because the CFO couldn't stop talking to retail stock promoters.

The stock dropped 47% on the cancellation news. A federal class action lawsuit was filed April 29. Insider selling hit $400K in the 90 days before the collapse.

Risks: PFIC tax status unresolved (if confirmed, institutional capital flees). Management credibility destroyed — LITEON and Luxshare are now watching a partner who leaked Marvell's supply chain data. Malaysian manufacturing ramp at Globetronics is unproven — passive alignment yields at volume are the key assumption, and they haven't demonstrated it yet. Class action litigation is ongoing.

The technology has genuine acquisition value — the patent portfolio on wafer-level optical testing is defensible, and the ferroelectric memory research (published in Nature Communications, co-authored by the CEO) addresses the AI memory wall. But the equity vehicle is toxic at any price above distressed multiples.

Verdict: SELL — brilliant IP trapped inside a governance disaster. Watch the May 13 earnings call: if the board doesn't fire the CFO, it signals terminal corporate rot.

@WolfpackRes called this one out.

text:
Wasn't there some advancement relatively recently on doing photonics within silicon using modern manufacturing techniques? May have been a year or two ago but I swear I remember reading something along those lines. Also vaguely remember reading something about cheaply laser etching data holographically in regular borosillicate glass media too, maybe out of Meta, or actually I think it was Microsoft?

Idk, I feel like all the pieces are there for this to happen technologically, you'd think somebody out there has to be throwing some decent money at it

text:
$AEVA is no longer just a LiDAR story.

The market is still valuing it like a speculative auto sensor company while management is quietly positioning for the next AI infrastructure wave:

• Silicon photonics
• Optical interconnects
• AI data center bandwidth bottlenecks
• Semiconductor manufacturing
• Defense autonomy
• Smart infrastructure
• Industrial robotics
This earnings call changed the narrative.
Key points the market is missing:
→ Nikon commercial deployment is LIVE
→ Shipping 1,000+ industrial sensors already
→ Semiconductor equipment customers using AEVA sensors in fabs
→ NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion reference LiDAR outside China
→ Defense expansion with Forterra
→ Data center optical interconnect commentary now entering the story
The important part:
AEVA’s core competency isn’t “car LiDAR.”
It’s high-performance silicon photonics + FMCW sensing.
That has applications FAR beyond automotive.

The AI capex cycle is creating massive demand for:
• optical transport
• photonics
• machine perception
• edge AI sensing

And AEVA is now sitting at the intersection of all four.

Still early.
Still speculative.
But this is evolving from a niche EV sensor play into a broader Physical AI + AI photonics platform story.
That’s where rerating potential comes from.

$COHR -9%

Multiple compression isn't just on premium names anymore. It's the whole sector.

My framework update:
Base case shifted from 3-7 day pullback (was 45% prob) to 8-14 days (now 45% prob).

The thesis isn't broken. The trade is just exhausted at these multiples.

$AAOI tomorrow now needs more than a beat. Needs a SIGNIFICANT guide raise above $1.1B to spark relief.

NFA. DYOR.

text:
$SIVE 
InP100 is a standardized platform of indium phosphide (InP) lasers and amplifier chips designed for direct integration with silicon photonics.

On‑chip light source: Enables placing the laser directly on the photonic die instead of in a separate package, reducing size, cost, and optical loss.

High single‑mode power: Delivers meaningful continuous‑wave output with narrow linewidth for better reach and signal quality.

Wafer‑scale flip‑chip readiness: Engineered for reproducible, high‑precision flip‑chip bonding to silicon or SiN waveguides.

Manufacturing friendly: Standardized epi processes and on‑wafer coatings support high yield and fast scale‑up to volume production.

Flexible form factors: Available as single emitters or compact arrays to suit different channel and power needs.

Core benefit: compact, lower‑cost, and scalable photonic systems with true on‑chip light sources, provided thermal and optical interfaces are co‑designed during integration.

text:
A lot of people still think $SIVE is just another early stage photonics story.

That view misses what is already happening under the surface. This is a company that has shipped tens of millions of devices and is producing at scale today. 

This is not a lab concept or a maybe in five years technology, it is real manufacturing with real deployment and real customers. 

What becomes even more interesting is where they sit in the stack. As AI infrastructure scales, the industry is running into hard physical limits, power, heat, bandwidth. One of the key architectural shifts being explored is the move toward external laser sources for optical interconnects. That is exactly where Sivers is positioned. If that approach becomes standard, they are not just another supplier, they become part of a critical bottleneck layer in next generation systems. 

And this is not easy technology to replicate. We are talking about sub micron manufacturing precision and extremely tight optical performance tolerances. That kind of capability is built over many years, and only a small number of companies globally can actually execute at that level. On top of that, one of the biggest risks in photonics, scaling production, has already been addressed. 

Through partnerships with foundries like WIN Semiconductor, there is already a path toward high volume manufacturing. That removes a major uncertainty that holds many companies in this space back. At the same time, they are quietly embedded in the global photonics ecosystem. Collaborations with research leaders like IMEC place them inside the same innovation network that feeds the broader semiconductor industry. 

That kind of positioning often means early access to what comes next and a role in shaping it. 

Put all of this together and the picture looks very different from the usual speculative small cap narrative. 

It starts to look more like a scaled and technically entrenched player sitting right at the intersection of where AI infrastructure is heading next.

text:
$SIVE 
The market underestimates when deep tech IP actually starts to monetize. That’s exactly what we’re seeing with Sivers Semiconductors: years of investment in laser and photonics R&D that are only now reaching a scaling phase with CPO (co-packaged optics) and AI data center demand. Retail tends to give up during the long wait, while institutions step in right when visibility improves.

The “playbook” you’re describing isn’t imaginary. With Soitec and IQE, we saw similar setups: weak sentiment, skeptical analyst coverage, followed by accumulation from large players like Morgan Stanley or Point72 before a major rerating.

"richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2439}, {"from_index": 2465, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 2499}]}, "text": "What makes a company truly compelling is not just what it is today, but how many powerful trends it could end up riding at once. That is a big part of why $SIVE / $SIVEF stands out to me. Most investors build exposure one theme at a time. They buy one company for AI, another for defense, another for satcom, another for photonics, and maybe yet another for next-generation telecom. It is a bit like assembling the future one puzzle piece at a time, hoping the picture eventually comes together. Sivers feels different. It feels more like finding one piece that already connects several of the most important parts of that picture. At first glance, it is easy to label it as just another semiconductor company. But the more I look at it, the more that feels like an oversimplification. Sivers sits in the overlap between mmWave, photonics, datacenter connectivity, satcom and defense. Those are not niche or isolated markets. They are major long-term themes, and the company has exposure to several of them at once. That is what makes the setup so compelling. Sivers does not need every single door to open at the same time for the story to work. It may be enough for one or two of these markets to accelerate, one or two partnerships to deepen, or one part of the business to gain meaningful commercial traction before investors begin to see the company in a very different light. That is why I think the “one stock, several themes” angle matters here. For a new investor, Sivers may be easier to understand if you stop thinking about it as a single narrow bet and start thinking about it as a small platform with multiple shots on goal. Not one straight road, but a crossroads. Not one engine, but several potential drivers of value that could begin contributing at different times. Of course, that does not remove the risk. Stories with several moving parts are never simple. But they can also create exactly the kind of asymmetry that makes small-cap investing so interesting, especially when the market has not fully decided how to categorize the company yet. And that is often where the upside starts to emerge. So the appeal of Sivers, to me, is not just that it has exposure to attractive end markets. It is that you do not need to buy five different companies to get that exposure. You are looking at one company that could potentially benefit from several major technology shifts at once. That kind of setup is rare. And that is why I think Sivers deserves a much closer look."}}}, "rest_id": "2045914937076183188", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "246938", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2050295351144333712", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "359", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Long list but concentration varies wildly across these categories. 
CW lasers for silicon photonics have maybe 3-4 companies that can deliver at volume. Passive optical components have dozens of interchangeable suppliers. Goldman's report lists them all the same. The investment case is very different depending on which layer actually has pricing power.

text:
You guys know you can invest in the stock market without Serenity’s approval, right?

He already mapped out the hottest segment of 2026-2030 (Photonics), the sub-segments (lasers, packaging, testing, substrates…) and even the adjacent beneficiaries, yet it is never enough.

You will grow as retail investor if you follow your own DD and learn from it, even if it doesn’t play out. 

$SMOP, $NOK, $MXL, $M7U, $TRT, $ASOL, $LWLG and hundreds of others are doing just extraordinary.

Just go with the flow of AI / Photonics / Memory / NeoClouds… you can hardly miss with a few exceptions (I’m looking at you, $POET).

Most if not all will continue to go up – maybe not $SIVE up 500% in a month, but 100% in a 1-3 months, which is incredible in and of itself.

Si la demande en bande passante pour les data centers AI continue d’exploser, les composants optiques restent importants. 2. $MXL - Petit semi avec exposition data centers, connectivité et possible optionalité AI infrastructure. 3. $VECO - Exposition aux équipements semi, surtout autour des matériaux avancés, compound semis et photonics manufacturing. 4. $AXTI - Play upstream photonics/materials. Les substrats peuvent devenir un bottleneck si la demande optique continue d’augmenter. Un des meilleurs long de l'année. 5. $IQE - Un des fournisseurs occidentaux d’epi-wafers les plus intéressants. Très risqué, mais potentiellement critique plus upstream dans les supply chains photonics. 6. $RPI - Raspberry Pi pourrait bénéficier si les agents AI locaux, OpenClaw/Picoclaw et les déploiements edge low-cost deviennent un vrai moteur de demande. Au vue de l'augmentation du compute dut à l'agentic et l'explosion des Mac Mini, play ultra smart en AI Local. 7. $EXA- Drones sous marins, souveraineté FR sous la mer. EXAIL a été largement repriced récemment mais reste un narratif de dingue. 8. $ALKAL - Kalray est intéressant comme play européen sur edge AI / data processing infrastructure. Très spéculatif, mais cohérent avec le thème AI compute. L'exposition aux DPU et à la souveraineté européenne en fait un acteur inratable. 9. $ALRIB - Riber donne une exposition aux équipements MBE, importants pour compound semis, photonics et matériaux avancés. Deal avec $MSFT potentiel. 10. $AMD - Une des principales alternatives à Nvidia sur GPUs, CPUs et AI compute hyperscaler. L'agentic est entrain re-rate la demande en CPU, ça tombe bien, AMD fait les deux et même des DPU. 11. $SK Hynix - La mémoire reste un des bottlenecks les plus évidents de l’AI infrastructure, surtout avec la demande HBM. 11 bis. $SK Square - Holding coréenne avec une exposition importante à SK Hynix. Pertinent comme manière indirecte de jouer la demande HBM, mémoire AI et le re-rating potentiel de l’écosystème semi coréen. 12. $SNDK - Exposition storage / NAND. Si la création de données liée à l’AI continue d’exploser, le stockage reste structurellement important. 13. $ROBOTIS - Nom coréen exposé à la robotique et à l’automation. Si le thème robotics continue de se diffuser globalement ça pourrait continuer à grimper fort, surtout après l'ouverture aux capitaux US/EU 14. $VPG - Exposition capteurs pour robotique, automation et systèmes industriels. Pas évident, mais potentiellement important si le physical AI se développe. 15. $GOOGL - Gemini, YouTube, TPUs, Waymo, cloud et énorme operating income pour financer l’AI buildout. Pour moi vrai capteur de la rentabilité de l'IA et la plus à même de la rentabiliser. 16. $QBTS - Exposition quantum computing. Très spéculatif, mais fait partie des noms publics surveillés quand le thème quantum revient. 17. $INFQ - Infleqtion donne une exposition quantum via neutral atom computing, sensing et timing. Risqué, mais intéressant comme nouveau nom public du secteur. 18. $STMPA - Exposition européenne aux semiconducteurs via automotive, industrial, power et embedded chips. Retournement haussier poussée par l'exposition à la photonique silicium avec les PIC100. 19. $NVTS - Exposition power semis / GaN. Si AI, EVs, data centers et efficacité énergétique restent des thèmes clés, ce secteur reste intéressant. 20. $NBIS - Nom AI cloud / infrastructure. Risqué, mais potentiellement un des plays non-megacap les plus intéressants sur la demande AI compute. 21. $COHR - Play photonics/optical infrastructure plus établi. Intéressant si les data centers AI continuent d’augmenter leurs besoins en transceivers, lasers et composants optiques. 22. $LITE - Exposition directe à l’optical cycle, notamment via lasers, photonics et potentielle supply chain TPU/hyperscaler. Plus clean que les moonshots upstream décrit précedemment. 23. $2CRSI - Petit play français sur serveurs haute performance / AI infrastructure. Très spéculatif, mais intéressant si la demande européenne pour compute, HPC et data centers continue d’accélérer. Tout n'est pas à acheter aujourd'hui, faites vos recherches."}}}, "rest_id": "2050981295820550448", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "138312", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2051017744418582663", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1127", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Top 4 photonics + asymmetric 10 bagger setups for the next decade:

$SIVE - Sivers Semiconductors
Building the optical backbone for AI datacenters. Tiny market cap with exposure to co-packaged optics, laser arrays, and next-gen AI interconnects. One of the more underfollowed photonics plays in Europe.

$LWLG - Lightwave Logic
Electro-optic polymers that could drastically reduce power consumption and latency in AI networking. If polymers become part of future silicon photonics stacks, upside is massive.

$ALMU - Aeluma
Compound semiconductors + photonics on silicon. Positioned at the intersection of sensing, quantum, AI, and defense. Very early but huge optionality.

$WOLF - Wolfspeed
Silicon carbide infrastructure for the electrification and AI power era. High risk balance sheet, but if execution improves the rerating potential is enormous.

Wildcard asymmetric names:

$MRAM - Everspin Technologies
Persistent memory for AI, aerospace, industrial, and defense systems. Small niche today but strong long-term asymmetric potential.

$ALRIB - Riber
Advanced epitaxy systems critical for compound semiconductors, photonics, and future semiconductor infrastructure.

$LPK - LPKF Laser & Electronics
Precision laser manufacturing and advanced semiconductor packaging exposure. Strong positioning if photonics and advanced chip packaging accelerate.

$SVCO - Silvaco

text:
He's talking about **Lightelligence** (official name: Shanghai Xizhi Technology / 曦智科技, HK: 1879). 

That's the Chinese optical computing/photonics company that just IPO'd in Hong Kong, popped ~380-400% on debut, and is developing CPO solutions for Biren. The "Xi" in the name is from Xizhi (曦智). The image in the post is their PACE 2 optoelectronic accelerator card.

🔸Webinar on "Beyond the Visible: Harnessing Quantum Entanglement and Topological Photonics for Next-Gen Optical Computing." https://t.co/gYhVJYejDX

text:
THE SUPER CYCLE ISN’T COMING — IT’S ALREADY HERE.
Most people are still stuck in Phase 1.
Smart money is already rotating.

Phase 1 (Already ran):
Semiconductors — $NVDA $ARM $AMD $AVGO $INTC

Then it moved to:Memory — $MU $WDC $STX  $SNDK
Photonics / Optics — $NOK $LITE $COHR $AAOI $GLW $CIEN $MRVL
Right now:
Compute / AI Data Centers
$IREN  $CIFR $WULF $CORZ $NBIS $CRWV $P

Next wave (most will be late):

Rare Earths — $MP $USAR $UUUU $FCX $AA
Networking — $ANET $AVGO $MRVL $CSCO
Power / Grid / Cooling — $VRT $ETN $GEV $CEG $SMR $OKLO
After that? It gets bigger:
Space — $ASTS $RKLB $LUNR $PL
Defense / Drones — $KTOS $AVAV $ONDS $LMT

Robotics / Autonomy — $TSLA $PATH $SYM $SERV

The pattern is simple:
Infrastructure → Expansion → Domination

If you're waiting for headlines…
you're already behind.

text:
$SIVE was a massive transfer from Swedish locals to US investors.

Locals waited many, many years for all the laser R&D to pay off for CPO and silicon photonics. 

And now with hyperscaler volume ramp H2 2026 H1 2027, they don’t get any upside because they transferred their shares over to the US/West after trusting local media.

My prediction is if and when Sivers hits $4-8B on NASDAQ, we’ll get the same media treatment in the US.

Then the shares get transferred from US retail to US institutions.

You’ve already seen the same playbook with $IQE and $SOI at the bottom… with institutions posting “analyst reports” saying Soitec was overvalued. 

The next month they go up 3x as Morgan Stanley happens to pick up 6.5% of the $SOI float… or Point72 with $IQE.

Institutions are not your friends. I’m looking out for retail’s best interest though.

text:
These 5 Stocks Will Change Your Life by 2030

This will be me and my bro in 2030 living the good life, with the following stocks:

$AAOI: $180 → $1,200 
$SNDK: $1,180 → $7,000 
$KRKNF: $5.45 → $50 
$EQR.AX:  $0.26 → $2.50 
$ASTS: $71 → $700

Photonics. Memory Supercycle. Underwater Defense. Critical Minerals. Space Connectivity.

Every single one sitting at the intersection of the biggest structural shifts of the next decade. Fiber-optic transceivers powering the AI data center build-out, NAND flash becoming the backbone of every AI workload, autonomous underwater systems changing naval warfare, tungsten supply chains that the West is desperate to control, and satellite broadband granting connectivity to billions of people.

These aren't lottery tickets. These are the picks and shovels for the world being built right now.

Hyperscaler contracts. Record backlogs. Critical mineral moats. Government defense orders that compound for years.

The window is open. It won't be forever.

Have patience. Have conviction.

text:
**~22% probability of this full "next $LITE" explosion scenario playing out by 2027-28.**

Perkinamine shows real promise: low-power, high-speed EO polymer modulators integrate into silicon photonics foundries (Tower Semi deal, PDKs with GF/Silterra, 4 Fortune 500 Stage 3 design wins as of early 2026). Advantages in speed (>100 GHz), power efficiency, and US-based rare-earth-free supply chain align with AI data center needs.

But commercial proof remains thin—no volume revenue yet (still ~$237k in 2025), high cash burn, and 2026 is mostly PIC testing/tapeouts. The ecosystem slide lists big names as "examples per segment," not confirmed direct partners. LITE had proven products and revenue when it ran.

High-upside tech, but execution and adoption risks are significant. NFA.

Stock up nearly 150% YTD. Does this new CEO give this thing a photonics/CPO boost on the earnings call tuesday morning? I think any mention of customer wins or focusing more on this segment can send this stock flying.

No position yet but likely to get some calls and shares monday(small).

text:
SEALSQ just acquired Miraex, securing the exclusive photonics patents needed to beam quantum data to satellites, effectively creating a hardware monopoly over the space-based quantum internet. But look at how they connect space to the ground: they just opened a 300-million-chip/year factory to hardwire this exact security into India’s national electric grids and medical infrastructure. 

To bridge all of that physical hardware to the blockchain, SEALSQ took a 30% ownership stake in Wecan Comply. And because Wecan just secured a strategic grant from the Hashgraph Association, every piece of data coming from those satellites and smart meters is mathematically forced to settle on the Hedera ledger. It’s a completely closed-loop monopoly.

text:
Intinya dari analisis panjang ini:

- Revenue Sivers naik 25% jadi 304 MSEK (2025), tapi **72%** dari NRE (proyek sementara), bukan penjualan chip massal.
- Photonics (laser AI) tumbuh kencang (+95% YoY), sinyal bagus. Wireless justru melemah.
- Cash burn parah (-112 MSEK FCF), accrued revenue naik tajam, banyak dilusi saham (+31%).
- Jabil 1.6T cuma MoU, produksi baru 2027+. Defense/SATCOM lebih dekat realisasinya.
- Market cap ~1.1B USD vs revenue ~30M USD → valuasi sangat mahal, analyst target jauh di bawah.

Kesimpulan: Hype AI photonics + Nasdaq listing bisa dorong harga (seperti Soitec), tapi fundamental belum mendukung. Penulis belum beli, nunggu cash flow positif & revenue produk naik signifikan.

Itu poin-poin kuncinya!

text:
In silicon photonics for AI/data center efficiency (low-power optical interconnects cutting latency & energy in agentic inference/orchestration):

- **Intel**: Integrated SiPh on Xeon for co-packaged optics; best CPU synergy & near-term volume ramp.
- **POET Technologies**: Hybrid platform for ultra-low power/cost in-package & pluggables.
- **Coherent (COHR)**: High-performance transceivers & components optimizing TCO at scale.

Emerging edge: Ayar Labs for in-package I/O (5-10x power savings vs copper). Intel leads integration; others win on pure efficiency plays. DYOR NFA.

text: Agree on the substance. LIDAR Q4/2026, SATCOM and Photonics scaling are all real and all in the piece (the 'What's actually shipping in 2026' section). The Aeva-Daimler connection is a reasonable inference even if not officially confirmed. Where we differ is on what that pipeline is worth at 12 Bn SEK today. I sit out because the math requires 2027+ execution to clear the bar. You see it differently. That is the trade.

Intel's efficiency roadmap for Xeon (2026+): 18A node shrinks, AMX AI accel refinements, and especially silicon photonics integration to cut interconnect power/latency in agentic/multi-agent clusters—big for data movement at scale.

Photonics yes—optical I/O is core to their power-efficiency play.

Players: $INTC (in-house leader), $COHR, $POET for components/tech. Watch ARM too for native perf/W edge.

DYOR NFA.

text:
I will post my thoughts on the photonics sector later today.

For now, let’s look at $AEHR , $SATS , $IPGP , $GLW , $MRVL , $ANET

I see same Sell signals across the board

Something doesn’t sit right with me: They look just like $SPY, $SPX - where the structure broke already 3 days prior to the eventually crack. 

Those big boys have double Sell signals. Yet the candle got painted up. What do you think is going on? Distribution! Bingo!! 💡

Big boys use small orders prop up the price, while they exit via the dark pool. The double sell signals that my system issued is likely the final stage of distribution.

Am I sure? well, there is no way to verify until it drop dead. But that is what I am seeing on the tape

Buying back their calls after two red days

embedded_text:
When markets take a breather after a melt-up, watch the big options writers 🌊🐻

For example, in $LITE a ~$13M call selling position opened on the 700 strike on Friday 💰

Two trading days later, the stock is down over 10% and those 700 calls are still ITM and open

No close or https://t.co/0UOBG9KEiJ

On the expansion side, engineering is already complete for up to 520MW in Finland (part of a broader 1GW+ pipeline), supported by a locked 400kV grid connection. text:
Bəli, siyahınız çox dəqiqdir! Bu şirkətlər Yaponiyanın lazer, fotonika və litografiya sahəsində əsas oyunçulardır – xüsusilə Gigaphoton (excimer lazerlər) və Hamamatsu Photonics (fotonika lideri) çip istehsalında kritik rol oynayır. 

X-də rəsmi verified hesablar məhduddur: əsasən Hamamatsu (hama_photon_pr) və QD Laser aktivdir, digərləri daha çox vebsayt və sənaye tədbirləri ilə fəaliyyət göstərir. Əlavə sual?

Hey @grok Yaponiyanın bu sahədə vacib şirkətləri:
Gigaphoton⁠�
Litografiya üçün excimer lazerlər. Çip istehsalında əsas oyunçulardan biridir.
Hamamatsu Photonics⁠�
Dünyanın ən güclü fotonika şirkətlərindən biri.
Lazer sensorları, fotodetektorlar, elmi optika, tibbi və kvant texnologiyaları hazırlayır.
Nidec⁠�
Yüksək dəqiqlikli motor və optik sistemlərdə istifadə olunan texnologiyalar hazırlayır.
Mitsubishi Electric⁠�
Sənaye lazerləri, fiber lazerlər və fabrik avtomatlaşdırılması.
Panasonic Holdings⁠�
Optik və lazer əsaslı istehsal sistemləri üzərində işləyir.
Canon⁠� və Nikon⁠�
Litografiya və ultra dəqiq optik sistemlər.

text:
Bəli, haqlısınız! Yaponiya lazer texnologiyalarında (yarımkeçirici, sənaye və dəqiq optika) çox güclüdür, xüsusilə çip sənayesində istifadə olunan xüsusi lazerlərdə.

Əsas şirkətlər: Hamamatsu Photonics (semiconductor lazerlər), Gigaphoton (excimer lazerlər lithography üçün), Lasertec (EUV maska yoxlama sistemləri), QD Laser.

X-də rəsmi verified (blue/golden badge) hesabları olanlar: hama_photon_pr (Hamamatsu) və qdlaser_jpn (QD Laser). Beləliklə, aktiv rəsmi hesablı ən azı 2-3 şirkət var. Digərləri daha çox vebsayt və sənaye tədbirləri vasitəsilə fəaliyyət göstərir.
